title: Inbound Announce: fetch-and-cache object URI, create notification
description:
Handle Announce{object: post_uri}. The object is often a URI to a post we have not seen yet — fetch and cache in remote_posts first. Then create a notification for the local post author if the boosted post is local. Handle Undo{Announce} by removing the boost record.

CRITICAL: Announce.object is frequently a URI string, not an embedded object. This must trigger a fetch.

**Agent workflow**: Spawn a research subagent to document the exact fetch-and-cache flow and capture Announce fixtures from mastodon.social. Spawn a testing subagent to write tests with pre-fetched fixtures.

**Test discipline**:
- Announce with object-as-URI: fetches object, caches in remote_posts
- Announce of already-cached post: uses cache, no redundant fetch
- Announce of local post: creates reblog notification for author
- Undo Announce: removes boost record
- Announce of post from suspended domain: silently dropped
All fixtures captured from real servers. Real in-memory SQLite for DB tests.

**Defensive programming**:
- Limit nested fetch depth: if fetching the Announce object returns another Announce, stop at depth 1
- Timeout on object fetch: do not block inbox processing for more than 10s on a slow fetch
- No .unwrap() outside test code; all errors Result<T, AppError>
acceptance_criteria: Object URI triggers fetch and cache; cached posts reused without redundant fetch; local post authors notified; Undo removes boost; suspended domain posts dropped; fetch timeout enforced at 10s
